reasoning_content: We need to determine if 389 is prime. A prime number is divisible only by 1 and itself. Check divisibility: by 2? No, odd. by 3? Sum of digits 3+8+9=20, not divisible by 3. by 5? Doesn't end in 0 or 5. by 7? 7*55=385, remainder 4, so not divisible. by 11? 11*35=385, remainder 4, not. by 13? 13*29=377, remainder 12; 13*30=390, so no. by 17? 17*22=374, remainder 15; 17*23=391, too high. by 19? 19*20=380, remainder 9; 19*21=399. by 23? 23*16=368, remainder 21; 23*17=391. by 29? 29*13=377, remainder 12; 29*14=406. by 31? 31*12=372, remainder 17; 31*13=403. by 37? 37*10=370, remainder 19; 37*11=407. Since sqrt(389) ≈ 19.7, we only need to check primes up to 19. Already checked 2,3,5,7,11,13,17,19. None divide evenly. So 389 is prime. Answer yes.
